steps.title: Find the decisive clue
steps.content: The phrase “without clear evidence” establishes that archaeologists lack definitive proof about what happened.

steps.title: Match the signal to the answer
steps.content: Speculate about means to form theories or opinions when evidence is incomplete—exactly what the sentence requires of archaeologists lacking clear proof. The other three—dismiss (reject), expand on (elaborate), and catalog (record)—don't capture theorizing in the face of uncertainty. That matches choice A — A is correct.
